underwriting_details.nature_of_your_business_activities_inclu:
Pawsitive Pathways Pet Care, LLC is a small, locally owned pet services company based in Oakland, California, serving primarily dog and cat owners within a 15–20 mile radius. Our core activities include:

1. Dog Walking (approximately 45% of revenue)
   - Daily mid-day individual and small group dog walks (1–4 dogs at a time) in residential neighborhoods and local parks.
   - Typical walk durations are 30 or 60 minutes, with occasional 90-minute adventure hikes in larger parks on weekends.
   - All dogs are pre-screened for temperament and basic leash manners; aggressive or reactive dogs are not walked in groups.
   - We use double-clip harnesses and safety carabiners, avoid off-leash walking except in designated fenced dog parks with explicit owner consent, and never walk more than 4 dogs per handler.

2. Pet Sitting and Drop-In Visits (approximately 25% of revenue)
   - In-home visits for cats and dogs whose owners are traveling or working long hours.
   - Services include feeding, fresh water, litter box cleaning, short potty breaks for dogs, light grooming (brushing, wiping paws), administering oral medications, and basic home care (mail collection, plant watering, adjusting lights).
   - We do not board animals overnight at our own premises; all overnight care is in the owner’s home.

3. Pet Daycare (Home-Based, Small Scale) (approximately 20% of revenue)
   - Small, home-based dog daycare limited to a maximum of 8 dogs at any one time at our primary residence in Oakland (zoned and permitted for home occupation; neighbors notified and no complaints to date).
   - Dogs must pass a behavior assessment, be spayed/neutered if over 8 months, and be up to date on core vaccinations (rabies, DHPP, bordetella) as verified by veterinary records.
   - The property includes a fully fenced backyard with 6-foot wooden fencing, secure double-gate entry, and separate areas for small and large dogs.
   - Dogs are supervised at all times while outside, and rest/quiet time is provided indoors in crates or gated rooms.

4. Basic Pet Training (Positive-Reinforcement Focused) (approximately 10% of revenue)
   - One-on-one basic obedience training for dogs (sit, stay, recall, loose-leash walking, polite greetings) conducted either in clients’ homes or in local parks.
   - We do NOT provide protection or bite training, guard dog training, or any type of security/police dog training.
   - All training uses positive reinforcement methods (treats, praise, clickers); we do not use prong collars, shock collars, or other aversive tools.

Additional Information:
- We do NOT perform veterinary services, surgeries, injections, or microchipping.
- We do NOT operate a formal kennel facility or cattery, breed animals, or sell live animals.
- We do NOT manufacture pet food or accessories; we occasionally resell small quantities of third-party treats and leashes to existing clients under $10,000/year.
- Transportation of pets is limited to pick-up and drop-off for daycare and vet appointments using our company minivan, with all animals secured in crash-tested crates or with seatbelt harnesses.

Website and Contact:
- Website: https://www.pawsitivepathwaysoakland.com
- Primary contact email: emily@pawsitivepathwaysoakland.com
- Main phone: (510) 555-2847

The business emphasizes safety, transparent communication with pet owners, and detailed written policies and waivers. All clients sign a service agreement that includes emergency veterinary authorization, vaccination requirements, behavior disclosure, and liability waivers. Staff members are background-checked, trained in dog body language and handling, and are required to maintain current pet first aid/CPR certification.
underwriting_details.number_of_years_in_business_and_experien:
Pawsitive Pathways Pet Care, LLC has been operating continuously for 4.5 years, since June 2021.

Owner/Managing Member – Emily Rose Hart (age 36):
- Has worked professionally with animals for approximately 11 years total.
- 2012–2016: Kennel assistant and later shift supervisor at a veterinary boarding facility in Berkeley, CA, responsible for feeding, cleaning, administering oral and topical medications under veterinary direction, and monitoring dog playgroups.
- 2016–2019: Lead dog walker and pet sitter for another local pet care company in Oakland, managing a route of 20–25 regular clients and training new walkers.
- 2019–2021: Independent contractor dog walker and trainer while completing additional coursework in animal behavior.
- Formal training includes:
  - Certificate in Dog Training and Behavior from the Marin Humane Society (completed 2017).
  - Pet First Aid & CPR certification through PetTech, renewed most recently in March 2024.
  - Ongoing continuing education via online seminars (Fear Free Pets, APDT webinars) at least 2–3 times per year.

Operations Manager – Daniel Michael Hart (age 38):
- Background in small business operations and logistics.
- Responsible for scheduling, client invoicing, vehicle maintenance, and implementation of safety protocols.
- Has completed basic dog handling and safety training under Emily’s supervision and holds current Pet First Aid & CPR certification.

The company began as a sole proprietorship in mid-2021 and converted to an LLC in January 2023 as the client base grew. We currently average 40–50 active client households per month and maintain written standard operating procedures for key areas (dog introductions, emergency response, incident reporting, and vehicle safety). We have not had any major claims or lawsuits to date, and any minor incidents (e.g., small scratches from play, minor stomach upset) have been handled directly with owners and veterinarians without escalation.
underwriting_details.does_the_insured_have_a_local_authority_: yes – Home-based dog daycare and pet services business operating under City of Oakland Business Tax Certificate #BT-2023-184729, issued 2023-01-05 and renewed annually. Zoning clearance obtained for home occupation use at 3278 Linden Grove Avenue with permission for up to 8 dogs on premises during operating hours (7:30 a.m. – 6:30 p.m., Monday–Friday). No health department violations or complaints on record as of the date of this application.

business_activities_and_revenue.pet_transportation_answer: One company-owned 2019 Toyota Sienna minivan used for client pet pick-up/drop-off, averaging 30–40 pet transport trips per month, primarily for daycare and occasional veterinary appointments.
business_activities_and_revenue.pet_transportation_percentage_of_revenue: 3% (counted within dog walking/daycare services; no standalone transport fees except small surcharges)
business_activities_and_revenue.products_sales_up_to_50000_answer: Approximately $7,500 in annual resale of third-party leashes, harnesses, treat pouches, and commercially manufactured dog treats (no private label).
